fields.description:
## Context / Problem

`docs/llm-framework.md` documents the four-pass contract and says projects may add code-review
criteria through the same overlay as plan-review, but it does not document the runtime contract for
how a project code-review criterion actually flows through the pipeline — the contract the other
five children establish. Without it the fixed behavior is undocumented and can silently regress.
This is a purely mechanical documentation change (no new testable behavior), the last item in the
stack.

## Approach

Add a "Code-review project criteria" section to `docs/llm-framework.md` documenting the four
end-to-end contract aspects delivered by this epic, each as its own labelled paragraph/subheading
so the contract is legible and testable aspect-by-aspect:

1. **Round A/B fan-in + logical id** — activated project criteria are fanned into the finder passes
   and each finding carries its logical `project.<name>` id.
2. **Effective routing in Pass 3** — the repository-effective routing map governs the deterministic
   decision.
3. **`criteria eval` resolution** — project code-review ids resolve and calibrate through the
   code-review arm.
4. **Configuration-error behavior** — an activated criterion with a missing prompt/eval spec raises
   a located error.

**Rejected alternative.** A separate new doc file — the four-pass contract already lives in
`llm-framework.md`; splitting the code-review project-criterion contract elsewhere would fragment
the single authoritative reference.

## Scope

- `docs/llm-framework.md` — add the "Code-review project criteria" contract section (four labelled
  aspects above).

(No `## Testing` section: mechanical docs change, no new testable behavior — the acceptance check
is a documentation-content assertion.)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/llm-framework.md` gains a "Code-review project criteria" section that documents **all
      four** aspects, verified by a content check asserting the presence of a distinct marker for
      each aspect (so a single generic sentence cannot satisfy it): run
      `rtk .venv/bin/python -c "from pathlib import Path; s=Path('docs/llm-framework.md').read_text(); import re; assert 'Code-review project criteria' in s; sec=s.split('Code-review project criteria',1)[1][:4000]; markers=['round_a','logical criterion id','effective routing','criteria eval','configuration error']; missing=[m for m in markers if m not in sec]; assert not missing, missing"`
      (exit 0 = all five aspect markers present within the new section).
- [ ] The section documents each aspect as its own labelled paragraph/subheading (fan-in, Pass-3
      routing, eval resolution, configuration error), verified by the same command finding each
      marker within the section body (not merely once in the whole file).
